text:
Ecoinvent is a research collaborator [cite: 32].
**collaboration_potential**: Medium.
They provide essential planetary data but are focused on reporting rather than building interactive governance play-spaces [cite: 35, 36].
**governance_model**: Global non-profit coalition, with WattTime acting as the secretariat coordinating efforts [cite: 32, 36]
**open_source_tools**: Open data platform tracking 745 million individual emitting assets globally [cite: 32, 36]
**community_size**: >100 collaborating organizations [cite: 32, 36]

### What They Do
Climate TRACE is an open-data coalition that tracks human-caused greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions with unprecedented detail and speed [cite: 32, 35].
By harnessing over 90 terabytes of data from more than 300 satellites, over 50,000 sensors, and artificial intelligence, Climate TRACE has created the world's first comprehensive accounting of GHG emissions based on direct, independent observation rather than self-reporting [cite: 32, 35].
The coalition tracks emissions from 745 million individual emitting assets—such as power plants, steel mills, ships, and farms—and aggregates this data into 2.7 million public sources [cite: 32, 36].
Led by former U.S.
Vice President Al Gore and coordinated by WattTime, the coalition aims to facilitate radical transparency, enabling governments, companies, and activists to verify pledges to cut emissions and hold large emitters accountable [cite: 33, 34, 35].
### Why They Matter for Planetary Governance Prototyping
Any robust planetary governance simulation must rely on accurate, real-world data regarding the state of the planet.
Climate TRACE provides the ultimate "omniscience" tool for the Spaceship Earth dashboard [cite: 34, 36].
In a governance LARP, players representing national governments or corporations could use Climate TRACE APIs to independently audit the emissions of other players, entirely removing the "fog of war" surrounding carbon accounting [cite: 35].
It shifts climate governance from a trust-based self-reporting model to a cryptographic, observation-based truth model [cite: 32, 35].
## Rainforest Connection (RFCx)

**Name:** Rainforest Connection (RFCx)
**Type:** Company (Non-profit 501c3)
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://rfcx.org/
**Location:** Global (California-based)
**Scale:** Deployed globally (Ecuador, Peru, Cameroon, Panama, Indonesia)
**Maturity:** Operating since at least 2017

### Key People
(No specific executives named in the provided text, but referenced generally as the "RFCx team")

### Partners and Collaborators
Microsoft AI for Good Research Lab [cite: 37]
Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute (STRI) [cite: 37]
Zoological Society of London [cite: 38]
IUCN NL [cite: 38]
KKI Warsi (Indonesia) [cite: 38]
Fundación Futuro (Ecuador) [cite: 39]
Amazon Conservation Association (ACA - Peru) [cite: 40]

### Domain-Specific Fields
**funding_sources**: Non-profit funding, philanthropic partnerships.
**key_projects**: RFCx Guardian System, RFCx Biodiversity Monitoring system, Barro Colorado Island CNN project, Mashpi Reserve deployment, Los Amigos ecosystem monitoring [cite: 37, 39, 40, 41]
**partner_orgs**: Microsoft AI for Good Research Lab, STRI, Zoological Society of London, IUCN NL, KKI Warsi, Fundación Futuro, ACA [cite: 37, 38, 39, 40]
**event_participation**: Ecoacoustics Analysis Workshops [cite: 40]
**publications**: "Audio Ark" datasets comprising tens of thousands of hours of acoustic data [cite: 39, 40]
**collaboration_potential**: Medium.
Highly specialized in bioacoustics and hardware deployment for immediate conservation [cite: 41].
**governance_model**: 501(c)(3) nonprofit operating via local on-ground partnerships and adaptive management [cite: 37, 39, 41]
**open_source_tools**: RFCx Arbimon platform (web console), Visualizer for spectrograms, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for species identification [cite: 37, 40, 41]
**community_size**: Engages extensive networks of local park rangers and indigenous communities [cite: 38, 41]

### What They Do
Rainforest Connection transforms recycled smartphones into autonomous, solar-powered listening devices known as "Guardians" [cite: 38, 41].
Installed high in the tree canopies of remote rainforests, these sensors capture the ecosystem soundscape to detect the presence of chainsaws, gunshots, and vehicles in real-time [cite: 38, 41].
This acoustic data is transmitted via GSM networks to local authorities, allowing park rangers to intervene immediately against illegal logging and poaching, vastly outperforming satellite imagery which detects deforestation only after it has occurred [cite: 38, 41].
Beyond enforcement, RFCx uses advanced Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) developed alongside Microsoft AI for Good to identify hundreds of animal species from audio recordings [cite: 37].
Their Arbimon platform allows local scientists and ecologists to conduct long-term biodiversity monitoring, measuring the success of wildlife restoration efforts and uncovering new species [cite: 37, 40, 41].
### Why They Matter for Planetary Governance Prototyping
Rainforest Connection provides the sensory nervous system for the Garden of Eden.
While Climate TRACE monitors the atmosphere from space, RFCx monitors the biosphere from the canopy [cite: 38, 41].
Their technology represents the cyber-physical coordination necessary for planetary governance.
In a simulation context, RFCx's real-time alerts and "Audio Ark" can serve as ground-truth environmental triggers, injecting dynamic, real-world crises (e.g., an illegal logging event) into the game space, requiring immediate governance or financial responses from players [cite: 38, 40].
## Naturskyddsföreningen (Swedish Society for Nature Conservation)

**Name:** Naturskyddsföreningen
**Type:** NGO
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://www.naturskyddsforeningen.se/
**Location:** Sweden
**Scale:** National with regional branches
**Maturity:** Very established legacy institution

### Key People
Erik Pihl, Sakkunnig bioekonomi och klimatfrågor i skog och mark (Expert in bioeconomy and climate issues in forests and land) [cite: 42, 43, 44, 45]
Kristina Östman, Tf avdelningschef klimat och juridik (Acting Head of Climate and Law) [cite: 44]
Jonas Rudberg, Sakkunnig hållbar metall- och mineralförsörjning (Expert in sustainable metal and mineral supply) [cite: 46]
Hedvig Schylander, Sakkunnig Klimat (Climate Expert) [cite: 46]

### Partners and Collaborators
Klimatkollen [cite: 45]
WWF [cite: 45]
ClimateView [cite: 45]
Våra barns klimat [cite: 45]

### Domain-Specific Fields
**funding_sources**: Memberships, funds (Energifond) [cite: 46]
**key_projects**: Bra Miljöval (Good Environmental Choice) ecolabeling, critical advocacy on the Swedish "reduktionsplikt" (reduction mandate), evaluation of forest carbon credits (e.g., Arbonics/Treebula) [cite: 42, 44, 45, 46]
**partner_orgs**: Klimatkollen, WWF, ClimateView, Våra barns klimat [cite: 45]
**event_participation**: Skogens Dag [cite: 43]
**publications**: Extensive consultation responses (remissvar) to the Swedish government [cite: 44], emissions policy evaluations [cite: 45]
**nordic_connection**: **FLAG: Nordic Connection / Sweden**.
